December, 2006 by and between

Telecom Tower & Infrastructure Pvt. Ltd. a Company “registered under the
Companies Act, 1956 having its registered office at Essar House, 11 K.K Mara,
Racecourse Road, Mahalaxmi, Mumbai - 400 034, hereinafter referred to as
"Infrastructure Provider’, which expression shal! unless repugnant to the subject or
context mean and include its successors and permitted assigns}, of the FIRST PART
acting through its duly authorized to sign this Agreement.
and

Bharti Airtel Limited, a company incorporated under the Companies Act, 1956,
having its registered office at H - 5/12 Qutab Ambience, Mehrauli Road, New Delhi
110030 (hereinafter referred to as "Cellular Operator", which expression shail unless
repugnant to the subject or context mean and include its successors, subsidiaries
including Bharti Hexacom Limited and permitted assigns), of the SECOND PART acting
tlhirough its duly authorized representative to sign this Agreement.

V-HEREAS
1. The Infrastructure Provider owns or validly possesses the Telecom Sites across
India and is engaged interalia in providing Passive Telecom Infrastructure and

related operations and maintenance services to various cellular operators on
snared basis.

2. The Cellular Operator is engaged, interalia in providing cellular mobile telephone
services by itself and through its subsidiaries including Bharti Hexacom Limited.

3. The Infrastructure Provider has approached the Cellular Operator with an offer to
provide Passive Telecom Infrastructure facility and services. The Cellular
Operator being desirous of availing the said facility and services has agreed to
utilize Passive Telecom Infrastructure facility and the related operations and
maintenance services at various Telecom Sites in the Territory as mentioned
hereunder, ,

PART I: GENERAL

1.1 NOW THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained in this
Agreement and other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and
sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the parties, intending to be
legally bound, agree to the following terms and conditions governing the
provisioning of the Services contemplated herein.

1.2 OBJECTIVES AND SCOPE OF SERVICES:

1.2.1 Infrastructure Provider owns the Telecom Sites across India and provides such
Telecom Sites including its operation and maintenance services on shared basis
to its customers. Also, Infrastructure Provider is willing to construct new Telecom
Sites at new locations in the given circles, which has sharing potential. Thus it
shall provide completely built-up Tetecom Sites with all necessary infrastructures
as per technical parameters mentioned in Schedule 1 that can be used by the
Cellular Operators or as may be mutually agreed upon by the Infrastructure
Provider and Cellular Operator, on a plug and play basis.

1.2.2 The Parties acknowledge and recognize the need to provide Services to
subscribers of the Cellular Operator(s) in the Territory. The Parties acknowledge
and recognize the need to provide services more effectively and at agreed _ cost
and that by entering into such an Agreement, the above mentioned objectives
can be achieved.

1.2.3 The Infrastructure Provider shall provide the Cellular Operator the co-ordinates
(Latitude and longitude) of the Telecom Sites where the infrastructure Provider
has its Telecom Sites.

1.2.4 The Cellular Operator would examine the feasibility of utilizing such Telecom
Sites Passive infrastructure on shared basis with other customers of the
Infrastructure Provider.

1.2.5 The Cellular Operator may also propose the co-ordinates of the proposed
locations of interest, where Infrastructure Provider’s Telecom Sites may not
exist. The Infrastructure Provider would assess the feasibility and suitability
including the sharing potential of such proposed Telecom Sites. The
Infrastructure Provider shail build such sharable Telecom Sites within the
mutually agreed time frame.

1.2.6 Infrastructure Provider shall own and operate these Telecom Sites which are
capable of serving operators at the same time, within the Territory.

1.2.7. The Infrastructure Provider shall be deemed and construed to be the Owner of
all the Passive Telecom Infrastructure that will be provided by them. The right,
title and interest including any Enhancements carried out therein by the
Infrastructure Provider, shall vest with the Infrastructure Provider. Wherein all
such Enhancements shall be at the sole cost and expense of the Infrastructure

Provider only. Enhancements in this context means the augmentation in capacity
carried out by the Infrastructure Provider to achieve increased sharing as may
be agreed by the parties.

1.2.8 The Infrastructure Provider shall be solely responsible for obtaining all
approvals/clearances/permissions from the local municipal authorities and such
statuary bodies/ministries for setting up of such Telecom Sites and such
Enhancements, at its sole cost and expense. Cellular Operator to provide all
assistance, if necessary in this regard.

1.2.9 For the avoidance of doubt, the Cellular Operator shall neither have, nor be
entitled to claim, by reason of execution of this Agreement, any title or
ownership in Telecom Sites and/or Enhancements which are acquired or
developed by the Infrastructure Provider falling under the purview of this
Agreement. The Parties further agree that the right, title and interest in the BTS,
access radio and all other active infrastructure equipment being installed, kept or
placed at the Telecom site by the Cellular Operator (as specifically identified for
each Telecom site), shall vest with the Cellular Operator.

1.2.10 Infrastructure Provider shall provide operations & maintenance of the Telecom
Sites on a 24x7 basis which will inter-alia include services such as payment of
rental of the site so acquired, filling of diesel in the generator, payment of
electricity bill, security services at Telecom site, maintenance of 24 hours of
generator back up and other services as are detailed in Schedule 1.

1.2.11 Each Party shall ensure that neither its employees, nor the officers and agents
violate the terms of this Agreement.

1.2.12 All the provisions of this Agreement shall be applicable to each Telecom site
under this agreement unless repugnant to the context thereof.

1.2.13 Any amendment and or addition to this Agreement or related document can be
made with the consent of all the Parties and shall be valid only if made in
writing, signed by authorized representatives of all the Parties.

1.2.14 This Agreement and all other related documents will be binding on and inure to
the benefit of the respective Parties’ successors and permitted assignees. The
terms and conditions of this Agreement shall supersede those of all previous
agreements, if any, understandings and arrangements whether written or oral
between the Parties and shall not be varied otherwise than by an instrument in
writing duly signed by all the Parties.

1.3 INTERPRETATIONS: The term ‘Agreement’ used in the general terms and
conditions for sharing of Telecom Sites shall mean this ‘Master Site Sharing
Agreement and all Annexure and Schedules in accordance with the terms of this
Agreement. The word Party shall mean either Infrastructure Provider or the
Cellular Operator and the word Parties shall mean both Infrastructure Provider
and the Cellular Operator/s. The Annexure and Schedules constitute an integral
part of the Agreement. In case of discrepancy between different parts of the
Agreement, the following order of interpretation shall be applied:

° For General governing terms : Master Service Agreement
® For Technical / Commercial Compliance: The Technical & Commercial SLA
attached herewith as Schedule 1 & Schedule 2.

Page 3 of 40

DEFINITIONS

1.3.1 Annexure / Schedule: Means the Annexures / Schedules attached to this
Agreement at the time of its execution and thereafter from time to time.

1.3.2 Base Station: Means a fixed radio transmitter / receiver station which provides
a link between the mobile base station and Mobile Switching Centre (MSC).

1.3.2A Commencement Date : Shall have the same meaning as set forth in Clause
1.4,

1.3.3 Fees: Means the monthly service charges as explained in Schedule 2.

1.3.4 Force Majeure Event: Means any event of earthquake, landslides, riots,
floods, terrorist attacks, insurrection by war, acts of god or other event of like
nature which is beyond the reasonable control of the Infrastructure Provider
claiming inability to perform its obligation under this Agreement.

1.3.5 Good Industry Practice: Means the exercise of degree of skill, diligence and
prudence which is expected from a skilled, experienced and reputed contractor
engaged in the same type of undertaking under similar circumstances and acting
generally in accordance with the prevailing laws, rules, regulations, codes and
industry standards.

1.3.6 Governing Principles : The Services offered by Infrastructure Provider shall be
governed by a separate Service Level Agreement, as per Schedule 1 & 2, which
shall be executed by the Parties simultaneously with the execution of this
Agreement.

1.3.7 Headings: The headings or the captions of the paragraph or chapters should not
be used as a guide to interpret the paragraph or chapter. They shall be for
reference only.

1.3.8 Licence: Means a Licence granted or having effect as if granted under section 4
of the Indian Telegraph Act 1885 and Indian Wireless Act 1933.

1.3.84 Material Adverse Effect : Material Adverse Effect shall means an adverse
effect to {a} the ability of any Party to perform its obligation under this
Agreement or (b) the validity or enforceability of the Agreement or the rights or
remedies of either Party.

1.3.9 Passive Telecom Infrastructure: Means Passive Telecom Infrastructure
comprising of tower, shelter, DG Sets, air conditioners, electrical, civil work etc.,
which will enable the Operators (Operators include other Cellular Operators) to
install their telecom equipments at the Site(s).

1.3.10 Quality of Service: “Quality of Service” is evaluated on the basis of uptime
availability of passive infrastructure (i.e 99.90% on a monthly basis).

1.3.11 Rollout Plan: Means the month-wise/quarter-wise schedule of rollout of
Telecom Sites or as decided by the Celluiar Operator in the Territory and as
submitted by the Cellular Operator.

1.3.12 Service Order: Means the document by which the Cellular Operator places a
firm order on the Infrastructure Provider for the Services at Site(s) in the form

Page 4 of 40

as specified in Schedule 3 to this Agreement. The Service Order shall inter alia
specify the location and exact Passive Telecom Infrastructure requirements at
the Site(s) and the fees for the same fixed in accordance with this Agreement.
On acceptance of the Service Order, the same shall become valid and binding on
the Parties.

1.3.13 Services: Means provisioning Site along with Passive Telecom Infrastructure
facilities built thereon and the related operations and maintenance services as
more fully described in the Schedule 1.

1.3.14 Severability: In case any of the provisions of the contract is stuck down by the
court, only that part should stop operating and ali other provisions should stili be
binding on Parties.

1.3.15 Shared Telecom Site: Making available the Telecom Site and /or the Services
at the Site by the Infrastructure Provider simultaneously to one or more
Operators.

1.3.16 Telecom Site/Site/Cellultar Site: Means the location / place from where the
Services are provided to the Cellular Operator and includes wherever applicable
any land/structure erection thereon.

1.3.17 Territory: Means the telecom circle(s) of India in which the Infrastructure
Provider provides Services to the Cellular Operator.

1.3.18 Understanding: The Parties acknowledge that the draftsmanship of this
Agreement was a cooperative effort by both the Parties who were represented
by counsel and that this Agreement shall not be construed for or against either
Party, but this Agreement shall be interpreted in accordance with general tenor
of the language in an effort to reach an equitable result.

14 COMMENCEMENT DATE

The Infrastructure Provider shall, in anticipation of completion of a Site (RFIE -
Ready for installation of electronic equipment) fix-up the Commencement Date
and send a written communication to the Cellutar Operator (“Notice of
Readiness”) at least 5 days in advance informing the Cellular Operator that the
Services at the Site would be made available on such Commencement Date.

In case, if the Cellular Operator brings to the notice of the Infrastructure
Provider any material defects in Site or the Passive Telecom Infrastructure, the
Infrastructure Provider shall promptly rectify the same to enable the Cellular
Operator to install its equipments. The notice of material defect shouid be
brought to the notice of Infrastructure Provider within 10 days of receipt of the
Notice of Readiness by the Cellular Operator.

The Infrastructure Provider shall be deemed to have made available the Services
at the Site to the Cellular Operator on Commencement Date of the respective
site or on such date when the Services are made free of such material defects, if
any. The Commencement Date shall be either the date as specified in the Notice
of Readiness or the date when the material defects are removed by the
Infrastructure Provider whichever is later.

On Commencement Date, an individual site agreement as per Annexure A shall
be signed by both the parties and thereafter the Cellular Operator shall be

INITIAL TERM AND RENEWAL AND LOCK IN PERIOD

The terms and conditions of this Agreement shall come into effect from 1° April
2006, and will remain valid for a period of ten years and if any Telecom site is
still under operation under this Agreement, each Telecom site can be used by
Cellular Operator for a period of 10 years from its Commencement Date and
each such site shall continue to be governed by the term and conditions of this
agreement. The Agreement as a whole or in respect of one or more Telecom
Sites may be renewed with mutual consent of both the Parties, upon terms and
conditions that may be mutually agreed upon by the Parties. However, the
Parties should endeavor to renew the Agreement in such a manner that renewal
process is completed before the expiry of this Agreement. Al! the clauses of this
Agreement shall come into effect from the date of signing unless otherwise
specified. Each Site shall have a lock-in period as follows :

then the Lock-in-period shall not be binding on the Cellular Operator.

If Cellular Operator wishes to exit the Site before compietion of the lock-in
period (early termination}, Cellular Operator will have to pay Infrastructure

REPRESENTATIONS & WARRANTIES

The representations and warranties set out in this clause shall survive the
execution of this Agreement and its related documents and both Parties
acknowledge that they have entered into this Agreement and other related
documents in full reliance upon the representations and warranties as mentioned
below.

a) The Parties are companies duly formed and validiy existing under the laws of
India and have all the requisite corporate power, authority and necessary
licenses to carry on its business and to enter into and perform its obligations
under this Agreement and other related documents to which they are or will
become a Party.

under constitute legal, valid and binding obligations, enforceable in

c) The entry into and performance of this Agreement shall not violate any

e) Both the parties are not party to any Agreement, instrument, charter or

f) None of the party to this Agreement has occurred and is continuing or will

g) There are no actions, suits or proceedings (including arbitration proceedings)

h) The Infrastructure Provider has paid all taxes and statutory dues required to

contested in good faith. And it has paid all statutory dues (including gratuity,
provident fund, ESI and the like) payable to its employees and there are no
arrears in respect of such dues.

1.7 RELATIONSHIP

1.7.1 This Agreement is on a Principal-to-Principal basis and does not create any
employee-employer relationship between the Parties. Infrastructure Provider, its
employees, agents and representatives shail provide services as independent
entity and nothing contained herein shall be deemed to create any partnership,
joint venture between the Parties or a merger of their assets or their fiscal or
other liabilities or undertakings or create any employment or relationship of
principal and agent between Cellular Operator and Infrastructure Provider and/or
its representatives, employees and agents.

1.7.2 At no time shail Infrastructure Provider represent to any third party that the
scope of its association with Cellular Operator extends beyond the scope of this
Agreement. Persons employed by Infrastructure Provider will be under the sole
and exclusive direction and control of Infrastructure Provider and shali not be
considered employees of Cellular Operator for any purpose.

1.7.3 Infrastructure Provider shall employ and issue its own identity cards to its
employees for carrying out its obligations under the Agreement. Persons so
employed shall have no claim either for employment or for any money against
Cellular Operator. All contractual and statutory payments, inciuding wages and
salaries to the employees of Infrastructure Provider shall be the sole liability and

1.8 NO CHARGE OR LIEN

This Agreement shall not create any charge, lien or right in favor of the
Infrastructure Provider over any of equipment installed at the Telecom site by
the Cellular Operator and all equipments placed by Cellular Operator upon any of
the Telecom site pursuant to this Agreement shall remain the property of the
Cellular Operator. The Infrastructure Provider shail have the right to create any
lienfencumbrances on any of his equipments kept at the Telecom site provided
any such action of Infrastructure Provider shall not disturb any service that it
provides to the Ceilular operator as contemplated in this Agreement.

1.9 RESPONSIBILITY OF THE PARTIES

1.9.1 Either Party shall be responsible for any willful action or inaction as caused by it
(Defaulting Party) which may lead the other Party to be the subject of any
investigation or inquiry by the DoT or any other governmental authority and the
Defaulting Party shall provide assistance / documents / witnesses / advise from
their technical staff to the other Party as may be required to successfully close
the investigation of DoT.

1.9.2 Both Parties shall be not responsible for any incidental or consequential damages
arising under this Agreement or resulting from damage to the property,
premises, or equipment of the other Party.

1.10 GENERAL CO-OPERATION AND GOOD FAITH

The Infrastructure Provider and Cellular Operator/s shall undertake to co-operate
and collaborate with each other in order to facilitate the implementation of this
Agreement, in accordance with its terms. All the Parties should at all times:

a) Display the highest degree of good faith towards each other in all matters
relating to this Agreement

b) Devote such time as may be necessary to ensure the implementation of the
matters contemplated in this Agreement

c) Use its best endeavors to ensure that the matters contemplated in this
Agreement are fully implemented in accordance with its terms.

1.11 FORCE MAJEURE

Both Parties should be excused and not be held responsible for performance of
its obligations under this Agreement in the event of force majeure circumstances
like civil commotion, fire, tempest, earth-quake, terrorist action, war, floods, any
action by the government materiaily affecting the execution of the agreement
and or any other act of god or other event of like nature. Either Party on
becoming aware of such event informs other Party in writing of such force
majeure event as soon as possible. If the force majeure event continues for
more than 15 days, an emergency meeting shail be called between the
Infrastructure Provider & the Cellular Operator to take note of the situation and
to take necessary action.

1.12 REQUISITION:

1.12.1 In case there is requisition of any/all! Telecom Sites for use by the central/state
government or other Competent Authority in India during the term of this
Agreement, then Infrastructure Provider shall within 8 weeks from the date of
such requisition make efforts to relocate to alternative Telecom Sites and
immediately notify the cellular Operator, so that the services of the Cellular
Operator are not disrupted.

1.12.2 The services at the new Telecom site shall be governed by the provisions of this
Agreement, including penalty for delay. Infrastructure Provider shall remain
responsible for the due compliance with all its obligations under this Agreement
even during the period of requisition.

1.12.3 Infrastructure Provider shall as soon as practicable after the end of requisition of
Telecom site immediately commence rendering of services to the Cellular
Operator at the previously operational Telecom site at the option of the Cellular
Operator.

1.13 INDEMNIFICATION

1.13.1 Infrastructure Provider shall at its own expense make good any physical loss or
damage to equipment of the Cellular Operator if such loss or damage is caused
by any willful negligence, willful act or omission by Infrastructure Provider. Any
damage to the equipment of Cellular Operator by any other Cellular Operator
operating from the same Telecom site shall be deemed to be damage by the
Infrastructure Provider.

1.13.2 Both the Parties shall defend and indemnify each other harmless from and
against all claims, losses, suits, actions or causes of action, including all
expenses of litigation, court costs and advocate fees, for damage by reason of
any willful negligence, willful misconduct or breach of statutory duty.

1.13.3 If the Recipient Party is made a party to any litigation commenced by or against
the other Party (the Defaulting Party) for any of the above reasons, then the
Defaulting Party shall protect and hold the Recipient Party harmless and pay all
reasonable costs, penalties, charges, , expenses and reasonable attorneys’ fees
incurred or paid or likely to be incurred by the Recipient Party in respect thereof.

The Defaulting Party shall also indemnify the Recipient Party in respect of:

a) Any loss suffered as a result of any representation or warranty made in
connection with this Agreement being found to be materially incorrect or
misleading

Db) Liability for any death, injury or damage to any person or property arising
directly or indirectly from the improper use of the Telecom site or otherwise

c) Any loss or liability incurred resulting from improper possession, use or
operation of the Telecom site or any other equipment.

d) Any and all claims, damages, cost, charges and expenses incurred or likely to
be incurred by arising out of any acts or omissions or breaches of this
Agreement, law, rules, regulations by the Defaulting Party or its employees,
agents or representatives.

e) Any tax, duties, levies, cess, costs or liability, penalties, which may be
demanded from the Defaulting Party during or after termination of this

1.14 ASSIGNMENT

1.14.1 This Agreement including any rights or obligations hereunder can be assigned by
any Party to its group companies, subsidiaries, and affiliates under intimation to
the other Party.

1.14,2 This Agreement including any rights or obligations hereunder can be assigned by
any Party to a third party with prior written consent of the other Party which
consent shail not be unreasonably withheld.

1.15 INSURANCE

Infrastructure Provider should procure and maintain during the term of this
Agreement insurance for all the facilities at the Telecom site, installed and
maintained by the Infrastructure Provider. A third party fiability insurance shall
also be taken by the Infrastructure Provider to cover any mishappening at / due
to the site / Passive infrastructure. Insurance of any equipments installed by the
Cellular Operator at the Telecom site shall be the sole responsibility of the
Ceilular Operator.

1.16 RIGHT TO INFORMATION

Infrastructure Provider shail provide the Cellular Operator(s) on demand a copy
of all approvals permissions, licences and any other relevant document and
renewals thereof pertaining to the Passive Telecom Infrastructure that are
mandatorily required for providing such services.

Cellular Operator shall supply such information and documents necessary for the
Infrastructure Provider to provide services under this Agreement.

in writing and should be first transmitted by postage, prepaid registered post or
reliable courier with acknowledgement due followed by facsimile transmission
duly confirmed.

1.18 NON-EXCLUSIVITY AND PRE EMPTIVE RIGHTS

1.18.1 The right that is granted to the Infrastructure Provider shall be non exclusive i.e.
the Cellular Operator shall have the right to grant similar rights to any other
Infrastructure Provider in the Territory.

1.18.2 Infrastructure Provider shall provide services on a non-exclusive basis to the
Cellular Operator(s) i.e. the Infrastructure Provider should retain its right to
provide services at the Telecom site to any subsequent / new entrant Cellular
Operators, after intimating subsisting Operators.

1.18.3 In case any change or modification is required at the Telecom site for providing
services to subsequent/ new entrant Cellular Operator that should be done in a
manner that would have minimal impact on the connectivity and network on the
Cellular Operators’s network or service. Prior to taking up such activity, the
Infrastructure Provider shall give at least 2 weeks prior written notice to the
Cellular Operator of any such change or modification.

1.18.4 Following such notice, both Parties shall discuss the impact of any change that
will have materially adverse effect on the services of the Cellular Operator(s)
and the necessary action to be performed for mitigating or correcting such
impact. All costs for the same shall be borne by the Infrastructure Provider

1.19 RIGHT OF FIRST REFUSAL

1.19.1 In the event of availability of additional capacity beyond two racks space (which
Shall be reserved for Bharti) at a Telecom Site governed by this Agreement, the
Infrastructure Provider shall offer the same to the Cellular Operator weherever
the space as reserved above has been fully utilized by the Cellular Operator.

1.19.2 In the event of availability of a new Telecom site not governed by this.
Agreement in the Territory, the Infrastructure Provider shall offer the same to
the Cellular Operator. Only in the event when the Cellular Operator refuse the
aforesaid Telecom site it shall be open to a new Cellular Operator.

In the aforesaid situations, the Cellular Operator shall within 10 days after its
receipt of the right of first refusai notice to give the Infrastructure Provider

written notice of its intent to exercise the ‘Right of First Refusal’. If the Cellular
Operator does not give the Infrastructure Provider a written notice of its intent

1.20 CONFIDENTIALITY

1.20.1 All information exchanged between the Parties and all information of technical
and commercial nature disclosed during the term of this Agreement shali be
treated as confidential and shall not be disclosed in any manner whatsoever in
whole or in part, except as provided in this clause.

1.20.2 The Parties may communicate confidential information in whole or part, received
under this Agreement to its subsidiaries/affiliates. However, the Parties should
undertake in respect of such communications that the subsidiaries/affiliates
concerned will abide by the confidentiality requirement of this Agreement.

1.20.3 The contents of this Agreement and all information provided by one Party to the
recipient in connection with this Agreement will be held in strict confidence by
the other Party except the information which

a) Is in the public domain through no fault of any Party

b) Was properly known without restriction, prior to disclosure by either Party

c)} Was properly disclosed by another third party without restriction on its use
and disclosure

d} Is independently developed by the recipient without use of confidential
information

e) Is required to be disclosed pursuant to any provisions of law, rules, and
regulations or under any legal process under intimation to the reciptent
Party.

1.20.4 The obligations of confidentiality pursuant to this clause should survive the
termination of this Agreement / Service Order for a period of 3 years.

1.20.5 Within 7 days of receipt of written request of the disclosing Party, the recipient
should (i) promptly return to the disclosing Party all confidential information
disclosed in a tangible form along with all copies thereof and (ii) promptly
destroy such confidential information (including all copies thereof) and certify
their destruction to the disclosing Party.

1.21 INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS AND USE

1.21.1 Nothing contained in this Agreement should be deemed to confer on either Party
any rights or license in the intellectual property / IPR of the other Party.

1.21.2 Neither Party will use the trade name, service mark, trade mark or logo or other
proprietary information of the other Party. No reference to the other Party
should be made in the advertisement, self-promotion campaigns and publicity
materials and or press release compiled by one Party without the prior written
consent of the other Party.

1.22 APPROVALS

1.22.1 Both Parties declare and assure each other that, ali necessary rights,
